title: mimulus glabratus var. fremontii
intro: Mimulus glabratus var. fremontii, also known as the Fremont's yellow monkey flower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Phrymaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral compounds. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and soothing respiratory issues due to its sedative and expectorant properties. Mimulus glabratus var. fremontii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote calmness and well-being.
